entities.description: figure 2 is a visual representation used to illustrate the processing of a new entity in a knowledge graph
kg refers to the knowledge graph where entities are processed  compared  and merged
e 9 is a new entity being processed and compared against existing entities in the kg
e 6 is an existing entity in the kg that shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 9
e 8 is an existing entity in the kg that shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 9
e 5 is an existing entity in the kg that shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 9
e   7 is the final merged entity resulting from the consolidation of e 9 and e 7
the similarity curve is a visual depiction  orange line  showing the similarity levels between entities
the gradient based selection process is the method used to identify high confidence matches between entities
the orange line is a specific visual element within the similarity curve mentioned in the text
a unique  high confidence match is the result of the gradient based selection process identifying e 7 for e 9
consolidated information refers to the enriched data resulting from merging entities in the kg

relations.description: e 9 is processed within the kg context
e 9 shows high similarity with e 7 and is merged with it
e 9 shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 6
e 9 shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 8
e 9 shows a sharp decline in similarity with e 5
the gradient based selection process identifies e 7 as the match for e 9
the similarity curve depicts the similarity of e 9 with other entities
the similarity curve shows e 9 s high similarity with e 7
the orange line is the visual representation of the similarity curve described in the text
the gradient based selection process produces the unique  high confidence match
e 9 is the entity for which the unique  high confidence match  e 7  is identified
the merging of e 9 with e 7 enriches the kg with consolidated information
e 7 is the component of the final merged entity e   7 that incorporates e 9
